title: Hurghada Red Sea Diving Adventure
details: Discover the breathtaking underwater world of the Red Sea. Hurghada is home to some of the world's most vibrant coral reefs, diverse marine life, and crystal-clear warm waters — making it a bucket-list destination for divers of all levels.
highlights: Daily boat dives to top Red Sea dive sites
Night diving experience at Giftun Island
Encounter with dolphins, reef sharks & sea turtles
PADI dive briefings with certified instructors

faqs.question: Do I need prior diving experience?
faqs.answer: No! We offer Discover Scuba Diving for beginners. Certified divers can join our advanced reef dives. All levels are welcome.

faqs.question: How warm is the water?
faqs.answer: The Red Sea stays between 22–28°C year-round, making it comfortable without a thick wetsuit in most seasons.

faqs.question: Is the Red Sea safe for diving?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Red Sea is considered one of the safest and most beginner-friendly dive destinations in the world.

title: Hurghada Island Escape
details: Escape to the paradise islands off Hurghada's coast. White sandy beaches, turquoise lagoons, and the warm Red Sea sun make this the perfect beach holiday.
highlights: Sunset cruise on the Red Sea

faqs.question: Is this suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of our most family-friendly packages. The calm lagoon waters and sandy beaches are perfect for children of all ages.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit Hurghada?
faqs.answer: Hurghada enjoys sunshine year-round. October to April is slightly cooler and ideal for water sports.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the island trips?
faqs.answer: Yes, lunch is provided on all island excursions. Breakfast and dinner are included at the resort.

title: Luxor Pharaohs Private Tour
details: Experience Luxor on a fully private, customized tour. Your personal Egyptologist guide will take you through the Valley of the Kings, Karnak Temple, and Luxor Temple at your own pace, with no crowds and no compromises.
highlights: Private Valley of the Kings tour (incl. Tutankhamun's tomb)
Karnak & Luxor Temple with expert Egyptologist
Sunrise hot air balloon over the West Bank

faqs.question: What makes this 'private'?
faqs.answer: You get an exclusive guide, private vehicle, and fully flexible schedule. You visit sites when you want, stay as long as you like.

faqs.question: Is Luxor suitable in summer?
faqs.answer: Luxor can be very hot in July–August. We recommend October–April for the most comfortable experience.

faqs.question: How many tombs can we enter?
faqs.answer: Standard tickets cover 3 tombs. We arrange access to additional royal tombs including Tutankhamun's for an enhanced experience.

title: Luxor Culture & History Journey
details: Immerse yourself in 3,000 years of ancient Egyptian civilization. This cultural deep-dive covers Luxor's most iconic monuments with expert historians who bring the stories of pharaohs, gods, and dynasties to vivid life.
highlights: Nile dinner cruise with cultural performance

faqs.question: Is prior knowledge of Egyptology required?
faqs.answer: Not at all. Our guides pitch their explanations to the group — whether you're a history enthusiast or a first-time visitor.

faqs.question: How much walking is involved?
faqs.answer: Moderate walking over uneven terrain at sites. Comfortable closed-toe shoes are essential.

faqs.question: Are children welcome?
faqs.answer: Yes — Luxor is magical for children. We have family-friendly guides who make history engaging and fun for younger visitors.

title: Aswan & Nubia Safari Adventure
details: Venture into the raw beauty of Egypt's deep south. From golden desert dunes to ancient Nubian villages along the Nile, this safari journey takes you far off the tourist trail into Egypt's most authentic landscapes.
highlights: 4WD desert safari to remote Nubian sites
Felucca overnight camping trip on the Nile
Aswan High Dam & Philae Temple boat trip

faqs.question: How do we get to Abu Simbel?
faqs.answer: We travel by road convoy (3.5 hours each way), departing at 4 AM for the sunrise. A flight option is available as an upgrade.

faqs.question: Is the desert camping safe?
faqs.answer: Yes. We use established, secure camping spots along the Nile and carry emergency communication equipment.

faqs.question: What is the Nubian village experience like?
faqs.answer: You'll stay with a local Nubian family, help cook traditional food, and experience an Egypt that most tourists never see.

title: Aswan Leisure & Nile Relaxation
details: Unwind in one of Egypt's most peaceful cities. Aswan moves at a gentler pace — the Nile here is wide and calm, the people are famously warm, and the sunsets over the desert are extraordinary.
highlights: Sunset felucca sailing around Elephantine Island
Traditional Nubian lunch with local family

faqs.question: Is this trip suitable for older travelers?
faqs.answer: It's one of our most relaxed itineraries — ideal for older travelers, couples, or anyone wanting a slow, enjoyable pace.

faqs.question: What is Aswan like compared to Cairo?
faqs.answer: Much calmer, quieter, and less crowded. Aswan has a small-town warmth and stunning natural beauty.

faqs.question: Can I extend to Luxor?
faqs.answer: Yes — we can arrange a Nile cruise or train connection between Aswan and Luxor as an add-on.

title: Sharm El Sheikh Diving & Reef
details: Sharm El Sheikh is home to some of the most spectacular dive sites in the world — Ras Mohammed, the SS Thistlegorm wreck, and the famous Shark & Yolanda reef.
highlights: Dive the legendary SS Thistlegorm wreck

faqs.question: What certification level is required?
faqs.answer: Open Water certification minimum. Advanced Open Water recommended for the SS Thistlegorm and drift dives.

faqs.question: Is the SS Thistlegorm dive suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: It requires Open Water certification and comfort at 30m depth. Our dive masters assess every diver individually.

faqs.question: Can non-divers join?
faqs.answer: Yes — the resort and beach facilities are excellent for non-divers. We can arrange snorkeling trips and spa access.

title: Sharm El Sheikh Sea & Island Fun
details: The ultimate Red Sea beach holiday in Sharm El Sheikh. Stunning coral beaches, clear warm water, island boat trips to Tiran Island, and world-class resorts combine for a truly relaxing and fun-filled week.
highlights: Glass-bottom boat tour over coral gardens
Quad biking in the Sinai desert at sunset

faqs.question: Is Sharm El Sheikh good for families?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — calm beaches, shallow water areas, and plenty of activities for all ages.

faqs.question: How is the nightlife in Sharm?
faqs.answer: Naama Bay has excellent restaurants, shisha cafés, and bars. Lively but relaxed.

faqs.question: Is the water safe for swimming?
faqs.answer: Yes, very calm and warm. Designated swimming areas are monitored by resort staff at all times.

title: Cairo Culture & History Discovery
details: Stand at the foot of the last surviving Wonder of the Ancient World. Cairo is a city of epic contrasts — millennia-old pyramids alongside a chaotic, beautiful modern metropolis.
highlights: Pyramids of Giza & the Great Sphinx at sunrise
Islamic Cairo: Khan el-Khalili & Al-Azhar Mosque
Coptic Cairo: Hanging Church & Ben Ezra Synagogue
Sound & Light Show at the Pyramids
excluded: Camel or horse rides at Giza

faqs.question: Can we go inside the Great Pyramid?
faqs.answer: Yes — interior access is available at an additional cost. It involves a narrow ascending passage.

faqs.question: Is Cairo safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: The main tourist areas of Cairo are very well-traveled and safe. We use only licensed vehicles and vetted guides.

faqs.question: How busy are the pyramids?
faqs.answer: We visit at sunrise to avoid the crowds. Early morning is by far the best time.

title: Cairo Leisure City Break
details: A relaxed, fun-filled city break in Cairo that mixes iconic sights with great food, shopping, and entertainment. Perfect for travelers who want to experience Egypt's capital without a packed itinerary.
highlights: Khan el-Khalili souk shopping & mint tea

faqs.question: Is this a relaxed pace?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is designed as a city break, not a rushed itinerary. You'll have free time each afternoon to explore independently.

faqs.question: What is the best area to stay in Cairo?
faqs.answer: We place you in Zamalek or Garden City — central, quiet, and walking distance to the Nile.

faqs.question: Can I extend this trip?
faqs.answer: Easily. We can add days in Cairo, or connect this to a Luxor or Aswan extension.
